area_description: Factuality and hallucination in LLMs are critical challenges for reliable AI systems. In addition to RAG approaches, there are mainly two bodies of work to improve factuality and suppress hallucination: knowledge internalization and hallucination suppression.

categories.name: Knowledge Internalization
categories.description: The model learns to generate factual answers by internalizing knowledge from training data, including pre-training/mid-training strategies, post-training approaches, and new memory architectures for factual knowledge storage and retrieval.

sub_topics.name: Pre-training and Mid-training
sub_topics.description: Internalization through pre-training and mid-training by feeding good pre-train data, studying where to store knowledge and when is the best time to feed knowledge.

sub_topics.name: Post-training
sub_topics.description: Internalization through post-training, where fine-tuning must be done carefully as it can trigger more hallucinations.

sub_topics.name: New Memory Architecture
sub_topics.description: Proposing new memory architecture for factual knowledge storage and retrieval, where knowledge is internalized in the model through LoRA adaptors, memory layers, residual memory, etc.

sub_topics.name: Knowledge Editing
sub_topics.description: Techniques to edit or update specific factual knowledge stored within the model's parameters without full retraining.

categories.name: Hallucination Suppression
categories.description: The model learns to reduce hallucinated facts in generation through leveraging internal parameters, fine-tuning for correct token selection, confidence-based suppression, and verification-based approaches.

sub_topics.name: Internal Parameters
sub_topics.description: Outputting the correct fact by leveraging internal parameters at different layers of the model with different attention mechanisms, or by reciting.

sub_topics.name: Fine-tuning Based
sub_topics.description: Outputting the correct fact by fine-tuning, such that the model learns to choose the right token among its internal knowledge based on confidence.

sub_topics.name: Confidence-based
sub_topics.description: Suppressing incorrect facts using confidence-based softening, selective abstention with low confidence, or explicitly stating confidence. Confidence comes from internal parameters or is calibrated through fine-tuning with consistency and ground truth comparison.

sub_topics.name: Verification-based
sub_topics.description: Suppressing incorrect facts with verification using internal knowledge, removing hallucinations that cannot be verified.

theme.name: Factuality Evaluation
theme.description: Evaluating factual accuracy of LLM outputs, including detection methods, metrics, and assessment frameworks for hallucinated content.

theme.name: Mechanistic Interpretability
theme.description: Understanding how factual knowledge is represented, stored, and retrieved within model internals, including probing, circuit analysis, and representation studies.

theme.name: Analysis
theme.description: Papers where experiments are conducted to evaluate the performance of baseline solutions, showing gaps and future directions.

theme.name: Benchmark
theme.description: Papers that introduce new benchmark datasets and evaluation metrics.

theme.name: Application
theme.description: Papers that show how to apply the techniques to a specific domain or task, highlighting its strengths and gaps.

theme.name: Survey
theme.description: Papers that provide a comprehensive overview of the current state of the art, including its taxonomy, advantages and limitations.
